strategy.name: 4HR Supply and Demand Swing Trader
strategy.description: Multi-timeframe S&D strategy based on base candle zone drawing (wick to wick), FVG as quality confirmation, and lower timeframe entry on zone close. 4H defines zones, 1H confirms retest, 15M triggers entry.

timeframe_roles.4H: Zone identification — base candle before impulsive move defines the zone (wick to wick). FVG presence confirms quality. CHoCH confirms the reversal the zone caused. This is the WHERE and the WHAT.
timeframe_roles.1H: Retest — price returns to the 4H zone on the 1H chart, slowing momentum and closing inside the zone. This is the CONFIRMATION.
timeframe_roles.15M: Entry trigger — price closes back above demand zone top (longs) or below supply zone bottom (shorts) on 15M. This is the WHEN.

strategy.bias: Determined through backtesting per instrument — no HTF bias filter applied at this stage

zone_rules.definition: A supply or demand zone is defined by the BASE CANDLE immediately before the impulsive move away. The zone is drawn wick to wick on that base candle. This is where smart money placed their orders.

demand_zone.description: The candle immediately before a strong bullish impulse move. Price left this area aggressively to the upside — smart money was buying here.
demand_zone.zone_top: High (including wick) of the base candle before the bullish impulse
demand_zone.zone_bottom: Low (including wick) of the base candle before the bullish impulse

supply_zone.description: The candle immediately before a strong bearish impulse move. Price left this area aggressively to the downside — smart money was selling here.
supply_zone.zone_top: High (including wick) of the base candle before the bearish impulse
supply_zone.zone_bottom: Low (including wick) of the base candle before the bearish impulse

zone_rules.impulse_requirement: The move away from the base candle must be strong and impulsive — multiple consecutive candles in one direction, or one very large candle. Gradual drift does not qualify.

fvg_quality.description: A Fair Value Gap within the impulse move (3-candle imbalance where candle 1 and candle 3 do not overlap) upgrades the zone quality. FVG confirms only smart money can create this imbalance.
fvg_quality.with_fvg: Zone has FVG — A+ eligible. Strong institutional presence confirmed.
fvg_quality.without_fvg: Zone has no FVG — A eligible only if impulse is still strong (3-4 consecutive candles).

zone_rules.break_of_structure: The impulse move must take out a previous supply or demand level. A push that does not break prior structure is a weaker zone.

freshness.fresh: Price has never returned to the zone since formation — highest probability, A+ eligible
freshness.used_once: Price has returned and reacted once — still tradeable but weaker, A eligible
freshness.invalidated: Price has closed completely through the zone — zone is dead, do not trade it. Repeated tests weaken zones significantly.

zone_rules.trend_alignment: Prefer buying from demand zones in uptrends and selling from supply zones in downtrends. Note: trend filter to be calibrated through backtesting.
zone_rules.slow_momentum_on_return: When price returns to the zone, it should slow down — smaller candles, reduced range. Price slamming into the zone aggressively is a warning sign — do not enter.

A_plus.label: A+ Setup — Highest Confidence
criteria: Fresh 4H zone (price has never returned since formation)
FVG present within the impulse move away from the zone
Impulse move breaks previous supply or demand structure
Price returns to zone slowly (momentum slowing, smaller candles)
Price closes inside the zone on 1H
15M close back above demand zone top (longs) or below supply zone bottom (shorts)
Minimum 2:1 R:R to next opposing zone

A.label: A Setup — Good Confidence
criteria: Zone has been used once but still structurally valid
Strong impulse move away (3-4 consecutive candles) even without FVG
Price closes inside the zone on 1H
15M close back above demand zone top (longs) or below supply zone bottom (shorts)
Minimum 2:1 R:R to next opposing zone

sequence: STEP 1 — Identify zone: Find the base candle before an impulsive 4H move. Draw zone wick to wick. Check for FVG in the impulse — upgrades to A+. Check that impulse broke prior structure.
STEP 2 — Wait for return: Price pulls back toward the zone. It must approach slowly — momentum slowing, candles getting smaller. Aggressive candles into the zone are a warning.
STEP 3 — Close inside zone: On the 1H chart, a candle closes inside the zone (between zone top and zone bottom). This is the confirmation the zone is being respected.
STEP 4 — Entry trigger: On the 15M chart, price closes back above the demand zone top (longs) or below the supply zone bottom (shorts). Enter on that 15M candle close.
STEP 5 — R:R check: Confirm the distance to the next opposing zone is at least 2x the stop distance. Do not enter if not met.
long_conditions: Valid 4H demand zone identified (base candle before bullish impulse, wick to wick)
Price approaches zone slowly — momentum slowing
1H candle closes inside the demand zone
15M candle closes back above the demand zone top
Minimum 2:1 R:R to next opposing 4H supply zone
short_conditions: Valid 4H supply zone identified (base candle before bearish impulse, wick to wick)
Price approaches zone slowly — momentum slowing
1H candle closes inside the supply zone
15M candle closes back below the supply zone bottom
Minimum 2:1 R:R to next opposing 4H demand zone
do_not_enter_if: Zone has been fully invalidated (4H close completely through it)
Zone has been tested more than once without a strong reaction — zone is weakening
Price approaches zone aggressively (large fast candles into zone) — do not enter
1H candle has not closed inside the zone yet
R:R to next opposing zone is less than 2:1
Would result in more than 2 correlated pairs open simultaneously

stop_loss_rules.long: Place stop below the demand zone bottom (low of base candle) with a small buffer. Buffer to be calibrated during backtesting.
stop_loss_rules.short: Place stop above the supply zone top (high of base candle) with a small buffer. Buffer to be calibrated during backtesting.
stop_loss_rules.buffer_note: Buffer protects against liquidity sweeps just beyond the zone. Do not place stop at the exact zone boundary.
stop_loss_rules.invalidation: If price closes a full 4H candle completely through the zone — zone is dead, this is a full stop loss event.

target_rules.primary_target: Next opposing 4H supply or demand zone
target_rules.secondary_reference: Fair value gaps within the move can also act as TP levels
target_rules.rr_minimum: 3:1 minimum required on all trades to accommodate the full TP1/TP2/TP3 structure

scaling_and_exits.note: Pure scaling-in system — no partial closes. All positions exit together via trailing stop.
scaling_and_exits.entry: Full size at original risk (A+ = 3%, A = 1.5%)

tp1.action: Add 50% of original position size (Test A) or 100% (Test B)

tp2.action: Add 50% of original position size (Test A) or 100% (Test B)
tp2.stop: Move stop up to TP1 level — locks in breakeven+ on original

tp3.action: Add 50% of original position size (Test A) or 100% (Test B)
tp3.stop: Trail stop activates from TP2 level — follows price, gives back less than full move

scaling_and_exits.final_exit: Trailing stop hits → entire position (original + all additions) closes at once
scaling_and_exits.test_plan: Run Test A (50% additions) first to establish baseline. Then run Test B (100% additions) and compare R:R, drawdown, and net P&L to determine if extra risk is justified.

stay_in_trade: Price wicks through the zone on retest but 1H candle closes back inside — liquidity sweep, zone still valid
Price moves against the trade but no opposing zone reaction has formed — hold
exit_immediately: A strong opposing candle pattern forms AT an opposing supply or demand zone — exit at market
Price closes a full 4H candle completely through the entry zone — zone invalidated, full stop loss
Primary target (opposing zone) reached — take profit or partial
re_entry_rules: If stopped out and price returns to form a new valid zone nearby with a fresh 15M trigger — treat as a new A or A+ setup
Do not re-enter simply because price came back — a new zone and new confirmation are required

risk_rules.correlation_limit: No more than 2 correlated pairs open simultaneously

scaling_into_winners.note: To be defined after base strategy is validated
placeholder: Only add to a trade at breakeven or better
Each addition requires its own valid zone and 15M trigger
Each addition sized at 50% of original
Total exposure on one trade cannot exceed 6% of account

compounding: After each winning trade, add realized profit to account base before calculating next risk
After a losing trade, recalculate risk from updated reduced account base
Review and reset account base at the start of each week

trade_log_notes: Record zone tier: A+ (fresh + FVG + BOS) or A (used once, no FVG)
Record whether price approached slowly or aggressively
Record whether 1H closed inside zone before entry
Record target zone and R:R at entry
Record exit reason (TP / opposing zone reaction / zone invalidation / SL)
backtesting_notes: Trend alignment filter (uptrend = longs only, downtrend = shorts only) to be evaluated per instrument through backtesting
Stop buffer amount to be calibrated per instrument
Zone expiry (how long to wait for a retest) to be evaluated — no hard rule set yet
TP1/TP2/TP3 structure to be defined after base results are in
Scaling rules to be activated after consistent profitability is shown
known_gaps_for_future_tuning: HTF context (weekly/daily structure) not yet applied — see memory file for details
Order block confluence not yet applied
Liquidity pool awareness not yet applied
Trend alignment filter to be determined through backtesting
